title: Agentfile
description: Schema for agentfile.yaml — defines one or more AI agent containers

version.description: Agentfile schema version (for human reference only)

governance.description: Global governance config — controls spending, human approval gates, and rate limits. Can be overridden per-agent.

max_budget_per_run.description: Maximum spend in USD allowed per run. The agent stops when this limit is reached.

budget_warning_usd.description: Soft budget alert threshold in USD. When spend reaches this amount the agent logs a warning and emits an event but does NOT stop. Set without max_budget_per_run for warning-only mode.

human_approval.description: Pause and require human approval before specific tool calls execute.

actions.description: Tool names that require approval before running (e.g. ['file_write', 'GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL']).

notify_url.description: Optional webhook URL to POST when approval is required. Supports ${ENV_VAR} syntax.

execution.description: Global execution defaults — applied to all agents unless overridden per-agent.

mode.description: 'direct' = standard tool-use loop. 'planned' = generate a JSON plan first.

verify_steps.description: After each tool call, a separate verifier LLM checks the result before proceeding.

durability.description: When true (default), the agent automatically restarts and resumes from the last checkpoint on crash. Set to false to disable. Requires persistence to be configured.

triggers.description: Global triggers — start the agent automatically on a schedule or via HTTP webhook. Use target_agent to route to a specific agent in multi-agent files.

type.description: 'webhook' exposes an HTTP endpoint; 'schedule' runs on a cron expression.

endpoint.description: HTTP path for the webhook trigger (e.g. /run). POST with {"message": "..."}.

cron.description: Cron expression for schedule triggers (e.g. '0 9 * * MON' = every Monday at 9am).

port.description: Host port to expose for webhook triggers. Defaults to 8000.

message.description: Message to send to the agent when a schedule trigger fires.

target_agent.description: Agent key that receives this trigger. Defaults to the entry agent if omitted.

agents.description: Map of agent-key → agent definition. First entry is the entry point.

metadata.description: Agent persona and instructions — composed into the system prompt at build time.

description.description: One-line description of what this agent does.

role.description: Agent's role (e.g. 'Senior Software Engineer'). Included in system prompt.

goal.description: Agent's primary objective. Included in system prompt.

instructions.description: Detailed behavioral instructions for the agent. Supports multi-line YAML strings (use |).

constraints.description: Hard rules the agent must never violate (e.g. 'Never delete files without confirmation').

runtime.description: LLM provider, model, and container resource configuration.

provider.description: LLM provider. Can be overridden at runtime via AGENTFILE_PROVIDER env var.

model.description: Model name for the chosen provider (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6, gpt-4o, gemini-2.5-flash-lite). Override with AGENTFILE_MODEL env var.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. 0.0 = deterministic, 1.0 = default, 2.0 = very creative. Override with AGENTFILE_TEMPERATURE env var.

resources.description: Docker container resource limits and base image configuration.

cpu.description: CPU cores to allocate (e.g. 1.0, 2.0). Maps to --cpus in docker run.

memory.description: Memory limit in Docker format (e.g. 512Mi, 4Gi). Maps to --memory in docker run.

storage.description: Storage annotation (e.g. 10Gi). Label only — not enforced by Docker.

base_image.description: Docker base image (e.g. python:3.12-slim). Defaults to python:3.12-slim.

warm_pool.description: If true, container is not removed after exit — used by 'agentfile up' warm pools.

tools.description: Tools the agent can call. At least one is required.

name.description: Local name for this tool (used in logs and approval gate config).

source.description: Tool source: 'mcp://<key>' for MCP servers, 'composio://<APP>' for Composio integrations (e.g. composio://GITHUB), './path/to/tools.py' or '/abs/path/tools.py' for local @Tool Python files.

actions.description: Composio-only: specific action slugs to expose (e.g. GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL). If omitted, all actions for the app are available.

governance.description: Per-agent governance override — takes precedence over global governance settings.

max_budget_per_run.description: Maximum spend in USD for this agent per run.

budget_warning_usd.description: Soft budget alert threshold in USD. Warns but does not stop the agent.

enabled.description: Enable human-in-the-loop approval gates for this agent.

actions.description: Tool names that require approval before running.

notify_url.description: Webhook URL to POST when approval is required. Supports ${ENV_VAR} syntax.

execution.description: Execution mode and step verification configuration.

mode.description: 'direct' = standard tool-use loop. 'planned' = generate a JSON plan first, then execute step-by-step.

verify_steps.description: After each tool call, a separate verifier LLM checks the result before proceeding.

max_steps.description: Maximum number of steps in a planned execution. Defaults to 10.

durability.description: When true (default), the agent automatically restarts and resumes from the last checkpoint on crash. Set to false to disable. Requires persistence to be configured.

on_step_failure.description: What to do when a step fails verification: 'abort' stops immediately, 'continue' logs and proceeds, 'retry_once' asks the LLM to retry.

verifier.description: LLM used to verify step results. Defaults to the agent's own provider/model if omitted.

provider.description: Provider for the verifier LLM. Use a fast/cheap model (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5-20251001).

model.description: Verifier model name. Override with AGENTFILE_VERIFIER_MODEL env var.

max_tokens.description: Max tokens for the verifier response. 128 is usually sufficient.

thinking.description: Pre-run reasoning step — the agent reasons about the task before using any tools. Visible in the run trace UI as an amber 'Thinking' node.

oneOf.description: Set to true to enable thinking with all defaults (agent's own model, 2048 max tokens).

model.description: Model to use for thinking. Defaults to the agent's runtime.model. Can use a stronger model (e.g. claude-opus-4-6) for deeper reasoning.

provider.description: Provider for the thinking LLM. Defaults to the agent's runtime.provider.

max_tokens.description: Max output tokens for the thinking response. Default: 2048.

prompt.description: Custom instruction appended to the thinking prompt. Overrides the default 'analyze the task' instruction.

triggers.description: Per-agent triggers (in addition to global triggers targeting this agent)

serve.description: Keep running and accept /invoke HTTP requests even without triggers or collaborators

collaborators.description: Names of other agents this agent can delegate to

volumes.description: Volumes to mount: string refs to global volumes, or inline VolumeSpec objects

environments.description: Named environment overlays (e.g. dev, staging, prod). Specify only the fields that differ per environment — everything else inherits from the base config. Apply with: agentfile build --environment dev

additionalProperties.description: Partial agentfile config. All fields are optional.

max_budget_per_run.description: Maximum spend in USD per run.

budget_warning_usd.description: Soft budget alert threshold in USD.

agents.description: Per-agent overrides for this environment. Use the same agent keys as in the base agents: block.

model.description: Override model for this environment (e.g. use claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 in dev).

mcp_gateway.description: Connect agents to a remote MCP Gateway instead of spawning local MCP server processes. The gateway aggregates tools from workers running anywhere — cloud or on-prem.

url.description: HTTP(S) base URL of the MCP Gateway (e.g. https://mcp.ninetrix.io). Supports ${ENV_VAR} syntax.

token.description: Bearer token for gateway authentication. Use ${MCP_GATEWAY_TOKEN} to avoid hard-coding secrets.

org_id.description: Organization ID to scope tool access. Defaults to 'default'.

volumes.description: Named shared volumes (local dirs or S3 buckets) available to agents
